detected_stack.notes: Detected from composer.json, package.json, route/controller structure, migrations, and the React/Inertia frontend files.

architecture.style: layered Laravel monolith with Inertia/React SPA boundary
dependencies: Inertia route responses to React pages
FormRequest validation for login and profile updates
DB facade access to sessions table
Fortify feature flags and password confirmation routes
risks: Validation drift when controller logic duplicates request rules
Dead controller methods when routes are not registered
React form state mutation patterns can become brittle

general.description: Login credentials are validated for required presence, string type, and email format before authentication.
Profile update validation now uses the shared request contract plus a separate file upload constraint.
Session termination requires current-password confirmation and ownership checks before deletion.

mandatory.description: `email` must be present and match an email format for login.
`name` must be present for profile updates.
`email` must be present for profile updates and must remain unique.

default.description: `remember` defaults to false when absent because it is read as a boolean flag.
`photo` is optional and only validated when a file is supplied.

input.description: Email format and lowercase requirements for profile updates.
Uploaded profile photo must be jpg/jpeg/png and <= 1024 KB.

business.description: Login brute-force protection uses a fixed five-attempt rate limit per email/IP.
Deleting other sessions requires a correct current password.

database.description: Profile email uniqueness is enforced against the users table while ignoring the current user.
Specific session deletion confirms the session belongs to the current user before removing it.

conditional.description: Photo validation only applies when a file is present.
Session deletion routes only work when the session driver is database-backed.

performance_findings.description: Direct session table deletion is efficient but hard-codes the database session driver requirement.
The profile form mutates Inertia `useForm` state directly before submit, which is brittle though not currently a measured bottleneck.

security_findings.description: Profile update validation drift was present before the fix because the controller duplicated rules instead of reusing the request contract.
Session deletion checks ownership and current-password confirmation, reducing IDOR risk.

integration_points.description: Inertia route props feed React profile/security pages.
Fortify password confirmation and 2FA endpoints are consumed by the React security UI.

issues.description: `ProfileController::update()` previously duplicated validation instead of reusing `ProfileUpdateRequest`; fixed to keep the server contract consistent.
`SecurityController::update()` was not reachable from HTTP routing; fixed by adding the missing route.

recommendations.description: Replace direct `data.photo = ...` mutation in the profile form with `setData('photo', file)` for clearer state handling.
Update the Security page title from `Profile` to `Security` for UI consistency.
